us_equities.prose: Stocks rebounded broadly after the U.S. and Iran signed an interim peace deal, with the agreement lifting sentiment across risk assets and driving broad participation including small-cap names.

cited_sources.title: U.S. Stocks Poised to Close Week on Strong Note

rates_and_dollar.prose: Treasury yields and the dollar rose after the Federal Reserve held rates unchanged in a unanimous decision under new chairman Kevin Warsh, with traders increasingly pricing in the possibility of a rate hike later in the year.

cited_sources.title: Treasury Yields, Dollar Rise on First Fed Move Under Warsh

commodities.prose: Oil fell sharply after President Trump signed a deal to reopen the Strait of Hormuz, with Saudi tankers immediately resuming passage through the strait and the IEA warning that a lasting resolution could trigger a major supply overhang. Gold eased alongside the broader reduction in geopolitical risk.

cited_sources.title: Oil Prices Fall to Lowest Since War Began After Trump Signs Iran Deal

volatility_breadth.prose: Fear gauges eased as oil supply concerns diminished following the Iran deal and the reopening of the Strait of Hormuz, reducing the geopolitical risk premium that had been supporting hedging demand.

cited_sources.title: Gas prices fall below $4 per gallon as oil supply fears ease after Iran deal

stock:SPCX.prose: SpaceX shares extended their post-IPO slide, leaving the average buyer since listing roughly at breakeven as the initial enthusiasm from the company's first week of trading faded.

cited_sources.title: The average SpaceX buyer post-IPO is almost under water after two-day slide
